narrative.canopy: Idukki is steep in a way the other origins aren't — mostly slope, rarely flat. The terrain here doesn't allow large machinery or clear sight lines across the growing area. What grows here grows because this particular combination of altitude, shade, and rainfall suits it precisely, not because it was chosen for convenience.
narrative.land: Almost nothing grows in Idukki at scale except what wants to be here. The soil is deep and wet. The canopy is deliberately intact — not because anyone decided to protect it, but because cardamom grows better underneath something tall. The trees above the plants are left standing on purpose.
narrative.hand: Harvest follows the plant's own timing, not a fixed calendar date. Cardamom produces ripening pods over an extended period — pickers return to the same plants multiple times through a season, taking only what's ready. The knowledge involved isn't written down anywhere. It's acquired through years of watching.
